designNotes: Composite canvas, 42x27 squares, three regions: (1) bottom-left x0-19/y8-27 = harbor overview (pier C1 + all five vessels' weather decks); (2) top-left piece x3.5-11/y0.5-6.5 = interior of the warship's stern cabin = C7 Throne of Spiders (aft/stage+throne at its south edge, stairs down to the lower deck at its NW corner); (3) middle hull x21.5-30/y2-25 = warship LOWER DECK (bow north); (4) right hull x32-40/y4.5-23.5 = warship BILGE level.

Overview vessel identification (matches C1 read-aloud): pier platform center (8.5,14); Goldenhawk C3 = NW tilted barge with deckhouse (x3-7.5/y11-17.5); Twin Tigers C4 = NE barge with TWO shingled huts and open mid-deck (x10-16/y12-17); House of Clouds C5 = west horizontal barge with single long roof (x0.5-6.5/y18-21.5); Dragon's Breath C6 = SE barge with roofed cabin (x10-13.5/y17.5-25.5); the unsigned warship (Eel's End, C2 deck) is the large center-south ship x6-11/y16-26.5.

Lower deck (middle hull) room IDs verified by furniture: C12 hold = bow crates/lumber (y2.5-5); C9 brewery = walled port room with 3 stove-cauldrons (x23-26.3/y5-8.5); C8 privies = port-wall stalls with seat-holes (x22.3-23.5/y9.5-12); C14 = starboard room with blue four-poster bed + dresser + chest (x27-30.5/y10-14); C13 = starboard room with single desk and NO chairs — Devargo moved them up to C7 (x28-31/y14.5-16.5); C10 brig = straw-mound room (x24-26/y15-17.5); C11 = black hatch + coiled knotted rope (x27-28.5/y16.7-18); C15 = white web-and-bones mass filling the stern (x23-29.5/y16.5-23). NOTE: an unlabeled starboard room with round table+chairs at x26.5-29.5/y5-8.5 (crew mess) has no area code.
Racooze layout mirrors some text directions: C13's spider-painted door to C14 is in its NORTH wall here (text says south). Treat visual identity as authoritative.

Bilge hull (right): no drawn division between C16 and C17. I assigned the dry webbed plank platform at the pointed top end (x33-39/y5-9.5) to C17 ('only a soggy section of hull remains in the center' of the collapsed cargo bay) and the open 2-ft-deep murky water with pier pilings to C16 (pin 35.5,14). The web wisps on the platform also evoke C15's web-choked den — if your implementer prefers, swap C17's pin to the bottom (bow) end; flag for review.
C4 enforcer is 'one of the four pier enforcers always posted inside' — if you want strictly 4 pier guards total, remove one pier token while the C4 token is active.

Day staging: Devargo holds court in C7 noon to a few hours past dusk (staged there with 6 hopeful thugs at the feast tables). After midnight he is in C14 — move his token for night infiltrations. C7's hidden trap door (in front of the throne, not drawn) drops to C15; enforcers from C2 reinforce C7 one per round on alarm.
Hopeful thugs are staged with the module's 'Korvosan Rioter' actor — the C7 journal page explicitly links Hopeful Thug to korvosanRioter01.
C15's 'giant dream spider' is staged with the module 'Giant Spider' actor (journal links the official Giant Spider; the module copy is the stageable equivalent).

Token sizes: Jigsaw Shark is Large (2x2, top-left square given); Dream Spiders Small; all others Medium. Dispositions: Devargo's people neutral (social encounter by design); C15 den and C17 shark hostile.

Pins B1-B6 on the left half; B7/B8 on the right half.

Ground-floor IDs verified by furniture: B1 = shop with meat-display window table (north) + L-counter (x13.5-17.5/y3.5-7.2); B2 = hall and long staircase descending south at x13.6-15.3/y7.2-12 (leads up to B7/B8); B3 = dark meat-hanging room west of the stairs with low blood-stained table (x10.3-13.5/y8.8-13.5); B4 = killing floor (x9.5-19.5/y13.5-21.5) with two water vats, NW hammer+bloodstain, east butcher blocks/salt barrels, SE cast-off vat, and the bloodstained GRATE at x11.5-15.2/y20-21.3; B5 = two earth-floored indoor pens with troughs (x16-21/y8.7-13.5); B6 = large outdoor stockade pen (x2-10/y6.5-15.5) with the straw-filled wagon/shed to its south at x4.5-7.5/y17-19.5.

Upper floor: B7 = north room with round card table + chairs (pin 37,5); B8 = south room with Verik's desk-bed (west) and paper-strewn table/chest (east) — Racooze mirrors the text's east/west. Verik staged beside the desk-bed; the Raktavarna token IS the silver dagger pinning a paper on the east table — keep it hidden/disguised until exposed (Tiny token).

The 3 Reefclaws sit on the B4 grate squares but are actually in the half-flooded sewer tunnel BELOW the grate (running east to the river) — implementer should hide them or note elevation; they only attack if the remains are disturbed (the severed-finger evidence is down there).

B4's Cow Hammer Boys are the 75%-chance-by-day pair (Parns and Karralo); on a miss they are in B5/B6 tending livestock instead. B1's pair are neutral-by-default (free-meat front); they raise longbows and shout to B4 for help if pushed past.
Verik (B8) dithers several rounds before joining any fight below and surrenders readily when shown the evidence; staged hostile but the encounter is designed to be skippable.

designNotes: Single-level 50x33 cave complex; entrance stairs from Potter's Ward mausoleum enter D1 from the north at x16-18/y2-7. Region guide: D1 ossuary NW (four pillars at x13.8-18.2/y9-12.5; WEST bone pit x9.7-12.8/y9-12 holds the 4 skeletons, EAST bone pit x20.5-23.5/y9-12 holds the owlbear); D12 and D13 along the north edge; D11 stitchery NE (x30.5-38.5/y5.5-12.5, patchwork body clearly visible on the big table); D10 library center (x24.5-30/y10-14.5 — Racooze draws THREE book/scroll racks vs the text's two); D7 alchemy lab (x27-32.5/y17-25, vial-covered tables + three black cauldrons on the east wall); D6 = the distinct skull-studded vertical corridor x24-26.5/y17-28.5; D8 store room (x26.5-31.5/y24.5-30.5) with its BOARDED door on the north side at x28-29.5; D5 exsanguination (x16.5-23.5/y18-23.5: three blood-stained tables + black wicker stirge hutch at x21.5-23.3/y18.3-20.5, plus a third blood table in its southern lobe at x20-22.3/y25.3-27); D3 derro cave SW (x4-12/y18-26, game table at x8-9/y20-21.7); D4 corpse dump S (x9.5-17/y25.5-32.5: dark mud ring around a solid central island, body-part pile with red debris at the island's south edge — Gaekhen's legs are in that pile); D9 prisoner pits SE (central rock floor x35.5-39.5/y21.5-26 ringed on three sides by dark 10-ft pits; NW pit contains the visible water trough).
Token sizes: Skeletal Owlbear, Otyugh, and Cabbagehead are Large (2x2; gx/gy = top-left square). Derros and Vreeg are Small; Stirges are Tiny.
D1: the skeletons pursue intruders anywhere in the warrens (never up to the surface); the owlbear NEVER leaves D1 and treats the pillar gaps/stairs as squeeze/difficult terrain.

D5: the 4 'Gorged' stirges sit on the fresh corpse on the middle table and ignore the PCs; only the 2 'Hungry' ones by the hutch attack. The D5 derro's first move is to flee through D2 to warn Vreeg (D13) — the chapter intro recommends combining D5/D3/D7 derros into one Low-Moderate fight if the alarm spreads.

D6: the two necrophidiuses start motionless inside the skull walls at the corridor's far ends (tokens at the north and south ends); the Acid-Spraying Skulls token is the hazards-pack actor for the corridor trap, placed mid-corridor — it fires two rounds after entry and rakes the whole hall.

D2 crawl spaces: pinned at the most prominent timber-braced crawl tunnel west of D5 (x13.8-15.8/y20-21); other concealed crawl segments exist around the complex (e.g., far west of D3) — they are the derros' flank/alarm network, each concealed door found per the D2 page.
D8: the carrion golem still clutches Gaekhen's tattooed left arm; opening the boarded door triggers it immediately.

D13: Vreeg is the boss (kites flying with rays; bright light dazzles him — derro sunlight vulnerability). His bodyguard uses the module Human Zombie actor; per the journal, if the PCs left Gaedren's body at the fishery this zombie IS Gaedren reanimated by Rolth. Gaekhen's torso+right arm twitch on the north bench (flavor hazard, not staged).
Derro headcount matches the journal exactly: Vreeg's four derros = 2 in D3 (Rat Squish), 1 in D5, 1 in D7.

skippedCreatures: Rois Vindmel (A4) — a drowned corpse/prop knotted in the bed linens, not a creature and not in actor_id_map
designNotes: Single 43x18 map, no floor composites. Layout verified by render: bow section (A1) at map-west x~2.5-13.5 y~4-13 (bowsprit pole at top-left, two splintered debris-filled decks); the large central rock the ship split over spans x~13.5-19.5 full height; stern section east of it: A2 Shattered Hold x~20.5-34.5 y~6.5-12.5 (identical floating boxes/crates at x25-30 y7-9), A3 Crew Quarters x~34.5-40.5 y~6.5-9.5 (four colored hammocks visible at rows y7 and y8.5), A4 Captain's Cabin x~33.5-41 y~2.5-6.5 (snapped canopied bed frame at x36-38, footlocker at the far/east end ~x40 y4).
Entirely underwater dungeon — journal flags PF2e aquatic-combat penalties; without water breathing/freedom of movement PCs fight at a real disadvantage. Flag to GM on scene notes if possible.
Token sizes: Silt Eel sm (1x1), Skinshear med (1x1 — actor map lists him med even though flavor calls him an unusually large jigsaw shark), Yvicca med (1x1).

Behavior staging: the 6 silt eels nest in A1's scattered pots/overturned bunks (wounded eels flee deeper into the bow and fight to the death there). Skinshear circles A2 guarding the way down and butts the A3 door to warn Yvicca before attacking. Yvicca lairs in A3 among the hammocks (gore-chum room); warned, she pre-buffs (Evil Eye) and joins the A2 fight.
A4 has no living occupant by default — only the Vindmel corpse and the sealed footlocker (loot per journal). Door to A4 is swollen shut and must be forced.

skippedCreatures: Eries Yelloweyes — mission-giver who approaches the PCs in the city; not present in the den and not in actor_id_map
designNotes: Region guide (verified on renders, 20x14 map): B5 = bone-littered cave across the top (x4-12.5, y0.5-3, white bone litter + hay); B2 = west fungus cave (x1.5-7.5, y3-9) with black-mushroom-ringed slime pool at its south end and a rock pillar near (5,6); B3 = central cave around the large bonfire (cave x9-15.5, y4.5-10.8, fire at x11-13.5 y5.5-8, hay nests in alcoves, sewage seep along its west edge); B6 = top-right den (x13-19, y0.5-6) with stacked crates, scattered weapons and the pinned Korvosa map; sewer channels at bottom: a diagonal brick channel enters from the west edge and forks/joins the east-west channel at ~(3,11) = B1; the east-west channel (below the brick wall at y~11) runs the full width.

B4 = the penned section of the east-west channel between the two rusty iron grates visible crossing the channel at x~5.4 and x~16.3; the otyugh's cart-sized filth clot is the pale mound at x~9-10.7 against the wall. The otyugh (lg, 2x2 — token at 9,12 covers 9-11 x 12-14) sleeps on/beside its clot until noise wakes it; it can be bribed/Diplomacized into helping against the wererats, so GM may flip disposition.

Shrieker is staged as a hazard token at B2's south-wall mushroom pool (5,8) — it screeches unless intruders immediately move north past the central pillar; the alarm summons the B3 wererats, brings the B5 rat swarm (dire rats are too lazy), and wakes the otyugh.
Rat Swarm is lg (2x2, token at 7,1 covers 7-9 x 1-3); Otyugh is lg (2x2); all other tokens med/sm 1x1.

Girrigz staged unwarned in B6 at (14,3), caught studying his map. If fighting erupts in B3 or the otyugh is released he arrives pre-buffed in dire-rat form through the B3 east-wall squeeze-crack (B3 NE corner ~(14.5,4.5)). He fights to the death; the other wererats are coerced kin — scattering the warband without killing them saves 400 citizens (nonlethal resolution matters; GM note).
Sewage-flow squares (B2/B3 west edges, all channel floors) are slick: faster than half Speed or attacking in the flow forces a save or fall, per the mission page.

skippedCreatures: Rodolfo Giotorri — desiccated corpse drawn on the map in D2 (~12.5,9); a prop with 8 neck-punctures and a key ring, not an actor
designNotes: Single 17x20 map, verified on renders: D3 toy showroom = north room of the building (x8.2-14.5, y2-7.5; belled north entrance at ~(12,2), toy shelves, dollhouse table at ~(13,4), 'Private' doorway in its south wall at ~(10,7.7)); D2 workshop = south room (x8.4-14.5, y7.8-12.8; doll shelves along west wall, workbench + the toymaker's bald corpse at ~(12.5-13.5, 8-9.7), rickety trap door against the EAST wall at ~(13.8-14.5, 9.9-11)); D1 = the cobbled alley along the west side, bending at y~14, with the heap of 40+ plague corpses piled against D2's west (wooden) wall at (6.5-8.5, 9.5-14); the 10-ft hole between alley and workshop is in that wall at ~x8.3, y10.5-12.5.

DAY STAGING (default): all four vampire spawn sleep in their Ustalavic-soil coffins in a 3.5-ft dirt crawl space BENEATH D2, reached via the east trap door — the crawl space is not drawn on this map, so the four tokens are clustered beside the trap door at (12-13, 10-11). Implementer/GM should keep them hidden until the trap door is opened or the alarm wakes them; by day they take a -10 Perception penalty, and killing one wakes the rest. The crawl space is difficult terrain for Medium creatures.

NIGHT variant: move one spawn to the D1 rooftop (20 ft up, overlooking the corpse pile, -2 to spot from the alley floor); if it sees anyone touch the bodies it shrieks and attacks, and its 3 companions arrive from D2 in 1d3 rounds.

The map shows alley mouths at both the north and south streets, but the journal treats Racker's Alley as a shadowed dead-end — the bent corpse-pile stretch (y9.5-17, heavy gore on the render) is the keyed area. Contact with the plague-dead exposes a creature to Blood Veil (GM note).
Vampire Spawn tokens are med (1x1). They bicker and split up one-per-PC rather than coordinate; sunlight/running-water weaknesses are tactically relevant — the alley is in constant shadow.

skippedCreatures: Two shop-girls (C1 sales staff) — non-combat commoners, no actor in actor_id_map
Solt Carmino — Vendra's planted shill browsing perfume in C1; no actor in actor_id_map (social encounter only)
Queue of customers / crowd — set dressing, no actor
designNotes: Single 17x20 map, two buildings verified on renders. TOP building = C1 the Lavender perfumery (interior x4.6-12.6, y2.6-9): wide front-window display table with bottles at top-center (7-9.5, 2.2-3.3), two round lace perfume tables at (6-7.5,4-5.5) and (6-7.5,7-8.5), the long counter running along x~11 (y5-8.7) with the clay-phial racks behind it on the east wall (x12-12.7), red shop sign on the west wall.

BOTTOM building = two mirrored apartments behind the shop. WEST apartment = C2 (x4-9.5, y10-15): purple armchairs and ivy-carved/lace tables in the salon, kitchen nook to the east (counter with tea service + curved hookah at top ~(6.5-8,10.2-11), back-to-back stove pair on the dividing wall), canopied bed with purple silk at (6.8-9.3, 11.8-13.4). EAST apartment = C3 the condemned liniment lab (x9.7-15.5, y10-15): broken crates everywhere, water casks and the vial/perfume-bottle box stacks in its west half (x10.3-11.8), and the oily mixing tub WITH THE OAR sticking out at (13.5-15.3, 13-14.6) — unmistakable on the render. The corner-hinged secret door from C2 into C3 is in their shared wall (~x9.6); C3 is otherwise boarded shut inside and out (Hardness 5, 15 HP to break the boards).

DAY STAGING (default): Vendra Loaggri works the crowd mid-shop at (8,5) with two sap-armed thugs watching from (5,4) (near the entrance/window) and (9,7) (by the counter); one more thug works the lab in C3 at (12,13) by the tub. All med 1x1, hostile-when-provoked — this mission is meant to be solved by exposure, not combat (full XP for foes not fought; GM may prefer neutral disposition until Vendra is cornered — she knifes a PC and flees toward C2).

NIGHT variant (not staged): no crowd and fewer guards in C1; Vendra + two thugs brew in C3, with a 50% chance Vendra is instead asleep in C2. Thugs haul Jeggare river-water barrels in by night — the easiest evidence to tail.
C2 has no default occupants by day (locked, DC to pick per journal); left unstaged.

Bedroom = room with the dark-quilt bed at (30.7-32.3, 2.2-3.2); the room east of the blood-pool landing is a BATHROOM (tub at 36.8-38.7, 2.3-3.3) — not the bedroom.

The 'E' getting-in pin sits at the west approach path/door; the manor has multiple entrances (west door, south door near the dining bay) and the book does not map which is the mahogany front door — west chosen as the grandest approach.

skippedCreatures: 60+ bedridden blood-veil patients (F2) — helpless innocents (2 HP), no module actor; the drawn cots represent them

designNotes: Canvas layout: LEFT copy (x 0-25) = pure ground floor (F1-F4). MIDDLE copy (x 25-50) = same ground image with the 1st-floor CATWALK overlay (F5): gray walkway bands at y≈10.5-11.5 and y≈19.5-20.5 plus perimeter strips; the BLACK rectangle (≈26-39.5, 4.5-11) is open air over the loading dock. RIGHT strip = 2nd-floor offices (F6-F8): the 2nd-floor tile sits at map-local x 35-60 but is transparent except its NE corner — playable rooms span ≈ x 50-59, y 0-21.5, offset exactly +10 squares east of the middle copy (its staircase at 55.5-58 aligns over the ground stair at 45.5-48.5 middle / 20.5-23.5 left).
Floor staging convention used: ground-floor occupants on the LEFT copy; catwalk patrol on the MIDDLE copy gray bands; F6-F8 staff on the right strip.

F1 = bottom-center reception (9.5-17, 19.5-23): long desk with scales at y 20.5-21 facing the south street door at ≈(12.5,23); Bhrunlida staged NEUTRAL behind the desk (hired charlatan — fights only if forced; story award for not attacking her).

F4 identified as the small NE annex room (15.5-20.5, 0.5-4) holding lockers and a rope-winch capstan at ≈(18.5-19.5, 1-2) beside the big diagonal staircase (20.5-23.5, 0.5-4.5). Best-fit call: the capstan platform = the concealed cargo lift (lever button-key carried by Davaulus; lift descends 40 ft to temple G1). Moderate confidence — no other candidate room exists.

F2's Gray Maiden pair guards 'the door to F4 and the stairs' — staged on the y=5 row at the ward's north end. The catwalk pair (F5 page; the same '2 more above' mentioned in F2) is staged once, on the middle copy. Total Gray Maidens staged = 6 (2 ground + 2 catwalk + 2 F6) — matches the 3-at-most-2-PC disguise shift limit text.

F6 = top of the 2nd-floor strip (50.5-58.5, 2.5-6.5) where the stairs arrive; the gazelle double doors to F7 are in its south wall. F7 = middle strip with 15 restrained-patient beds lining both walls + central alchemy worktables (53.5-55.5, 7.5-12.5); physicians staged in the aisles. F8 = bottom (50.5-58.5, 14.5-21.5): bookcases/lab shelving west, Davaulus's desk at (52-54, 20.5-22) — he is staged beside it; on alarm he casts invisibility and runs for the F4 lift (escape route note for the GM).
Wall between F7 and F8 is visually faint on the Racooze strip (~y 14.5-15); pins are placed well inside each zone regardless.

Default posture is NON-hostile cover: physicians fight only if attacked / the cult is named / someone enters F4; only the Gray Maidens intercept by force. Dispositions are set hostile for staging convenience, but the GM should run the social layer first.

Alert-posture reinforcements (cultists massing at G8/G13, skeletons+zombies posted to G4, Gray Maidens covering G1) — conditional redeployments of already-staged tokens, intentionally not staged
designNotes: Single-image battlemap (54x23). Room key as identified on renders: G1 entry hall (2.5-8.5, 7.5-12.5) with the LIFT in the west alcove (0.5-2.5, 9-11); G2 guard post (4.5-8.5, 12.5-17 — central crate-table with mismatched chairs); G3 (3-9, 2.5-6.5 — long low bench; lower-confidence ID, by elimination); G4 = the glass-floored display corridor (9-15.5, 7.5-10.5) between G1's warded east double doors and G8 (dark panes = the undead memento-mori under glass); G5 operating room = the south hall (10-15.5, 11.5-19.5) with slatted strap-cots along both sides and blood marks; G6 = the cell room (7-12, 18-23) ringed by ~8 wall niches (the cramped cells); G7 cult quarters = the north room (9-15.5, 1-6) with 8 black satin-sheeted cots, footlocker row and table; G8 blood vats = the big hall (16-26, 6-14.5) with THREE huge vats (two amber at ~(20.5,8) and ~(20.5,12.5), one red at ~(24.6,10.2)) and a dark catwalk ring 10 ft up; G9 = barrel storage (16.5-21, 13-15.5) directly south of G8; G10 = the empty red-earth room (12.5-15, 19.5-22.5) (unfinished floor/rubble); G11 lab (20.5-26, 0.5-5.5) with the ogre-sized operating table at (21-23.5, 2.3-3.7); G12 (15.5-19.5, 0.5-5.5) books + green/black-canopied bed (the bed conceals his travel coffin); G13 (26-32.5, 6-14) with FOUR green glass tube-vats in a 2x2 at (26.8-31.3, 6.8-13.4) and N/S workspace alcoves; stone doors at x≈32.5 open onto the 100-ft sloping hall (x 33-41, y 9-11) down to G14, the domed rotunda centered (46, 9.5) — statue pool at center, the seven scourge basins are the colored circles on the rim.

CAVEAT — G5/G7 assignment is a judgment call; the Racooze fan map flips some book directions (book: G6 cells lie EAST of G5; here they are southwest of it). I keyed the NORTH bed-room as G7 (black satin cots + footlockers, and it is directly adjacent to G3, matching 'noise in G3 brings the G7 cultists', and directly north of G4 for the alert posting of its skeletons) and the SOUTH hall as G5 (restraint-slat cots, adjacent to both G6's cells and G8 for Rolth's alert move to the catwalk). If the implementer prefers the book's literal '8 beds = G5' reading, swap the G5/G7 pins and their token groups wholesale.

Death's Breath Doors: ONE hazard actor wards TWO doors (G1 north door ≈(5,7) and east double doors ≈(8,9)) — two tokens of the same hazard actor staged, one per warded door. A 3-action channeled heal (10+ vitality) suppresses a door's ward for 1 minute.

Caged Leukodaemon is LARGE (2x2): token at (27,7) covers the top-left glass tube exactly. It is imprisoned until the tube (Hardness 1, HP 2) breaks and is hostile to EVERYONE once free; recommend marking it hidden until released. Which of the four tubes holds the live daemon is the GM's choice — the read-aloud only says one form twitches.

Ramoska Arkminos staged NEUTRAL — a level-15 foe who explicitly wants no fight (bargain for Ruan: ~200 gp asking / 100 gp floor, or fetch the unused death's head coffer from G14). Ruan Mirukova staged FRIENDLY at (22,3) ON the operating table (unconscious, 0 HP, crushing-lever trap at slot 10 of 20 — see page). G12 has no default occupant.

Cultist headcount check: 4 (G7) + 2 (G5) + 6 (G8) + 2 (G13) = 14 staged, matching the page's '14 active cultists'. Physicians: 4 (G2) + 2 (G5) + 3 (G7) staged per room text (the alert text's '5 active' is a redeployment count, not a contradiction to resolve at staging time).

Journal links for generic undead (Human Zombie, Human Skeleton, Juju Zombie, Leukodaemon) point at official-compendium UUIDs, but same-name module actors exist in the id map and were staged from it. All tokens are med 1x1 except the Leukodaemon (lg 2x2).

G7 and G5 are furniture-dense; tokens were placed in the open aisles where possible — a few skeleton/physician tokens sit on cot edges by necessity (GM may nudge). G1 and G9-G12 are DARK areas per the page (lighting note for the implementer).

designNotes: CANVAS LAYOUT (105x28 map-local squares; Foundry scene px = gx*100+2700, gy*100+700 due to 0.25 padding): five 21x28 panels side by side. Panel 1 (x 0-21) = labyrinth orientation 1, the printed start state — the ONLY one allowing entry from E2. Panel 2 (x 21-42) = after one lever pull, panel 3 (x 42-63) = two pulls, panel 4 (x 63-84) = three pulls; a fourth pull returns to orientation 1. Panel 5 (x 84-105) = the lower level (E21 torture chamber, E22 torturer's room, E23 Gizzard). ALL pins and tokens are staged on panel 1 + panel 5.

ROTATION-TILE STRUCTURE: each level-one panel = the same ground image (static perimeter: E20 throne NW, E16 cross-room N, E13 octagon NE, E11/E12 obstacle corridor E, E10 well room SE, E1/E2/E3 entrance hall S, sea-cave + stairs-down SW) plus FOUR 8x8-square overlay tiles forming the rotating hub (hub spans x 1-16, y 6-21 panel-relative; section slots at (1,6),(8,6),(1,13),(8,13); pivot bearings visible as gray orbs at (5,10),(12,10),(5,17),(12,17)). Panel 1 tiles are unrotated. Panel 2: TL +90, TR -90, BL -90, BR +90 (adjacent sections counter-rotate like meshed gears). Panel 3: all 180. Panel 4: TL -90, BL +90, TR +90, BR -90.

CRITICAL TILE-ANCHOR BUG TO AVOID: in Racooze's original scene the 12 ROTATED hub tiles (panels 2-4) carry texture.anchorX=0.5/anchorY=0.5, i.e. their x,y is the tile CENTER, not top-left (e.g. tile at scene x=5300,y=1700 actually spans 4900-5700, 1300-2100). Our scene_brief/racooze_scenes.json extraction DROPPED the anchor fields — if the build writes these tiles as plain top-left coords the hub sections of panels 2-4 land 4 squares right+down (verified by render: they spill over the E11 corridor and leave the hub placeholder exposed). Either preserve texture.anchorX/anchorY=0.5 on those 12 tiles or subtract 400px from each of their x and y. Panel 1's four tiles (rot 0, no anchors) are correct as-is.

GM OPERATION: any rotation lever (E3, E8, E14 hidden, E16 east lever, E17) turns all four sections 90 deg and locks 1 minute. To run it, move party tokens to the SAME relative square on the next panel (+2100 px x; from panel 4 wrap back -6300 px). Tokens standing ON a rotating section additionally turn 90 deg about that section's pivot orb. The journal also blesses a static fallback (connect E3-E4, E14-E15, E17-E18 and ignore rotation) — then panels 2-4 can be ignored entirely.

ROOM-ID CONFIDENCE: high for E1/E2 (bronze doors + flanking tiger statues seen at (4.2-6.0,21.9) and (3.0-3.7/6.3-7.0, 21.0-21.9)), E7 (three chests in a row at (10.4-13.4,13.4-14.1); left=west Terinav decoy, middle=potions+Blood Cobra, right=east insanity-mist bellows — 'left/right' read facing the chests from inside the room looking north), E10 (central well + many-armed serpent statue), E11 (8 iron caskets in 4 alcove cells flanking the corridor), E12 (giant wasp floor mosaic), E13 (black faceted stone in octagon), E16 (four mist-sphere alcoves: black NW, gold NE, white SW, green SE), E20 (dais+green throne+blood; Vencarlo chained to the WESTERN statue), E21-E23. MEDIUM/GUESS: E4 vs E5 — green glowing pool nearest the entrance seam keyed as E4 'crystal' poisoned pool, dark blue pool keyed E5 healing pool (swap if you read 'crystal'=blue); E14 (TR section room with large skeleton + withered tendrils) vs E17 (TL section room with skull pile, cage and broken bed) could be swapped; E15 = small TR chamber (murals not discernible at map zoom); E8 = BR-section east room; E18 = TL section's main hall (opens onto E20's corridor in orientation 1), E19 = TL's crooked west arm — journal only says both halls rotate open onto Sivit's chamber.

TOKEN SIZES: Sivit lg = 2x2 (staged top-left (3,3) beside the dais); Rajambari huge = 3x3 each, staged at the four Gizzard wheel pillars, disposition NEUTRAL because they are passive unless they or the machine are harmed (all four fight to destruction once provoked; destroying 2+ locks the labyrinth's configuration). All hazards med 1x1.

ROAMERS: Vimanda staged at her patrol start E13 (disguised as a starved 'Vencarlo'; tracks Bahor's gifted ring; immune to the symbol glyphs; terrified of Sivit). If the PCs reach E21 without alarms or Bahor's deal, MOVE her to E21 to fight beside Senshiir (journal E21 alternative). Sivit roams/scries but lairs at E20 and wand-doors back there when bloodied — staged at the throne.

E16 mist-sphere teleports (west lever starts DOWN = every sphere dumps into a random locked E21 cell on panel 5): when up — white sphere to E2, black to E13, green to E20, gold to E5. The E9 'invisible arcane eye' watcher is a sensor, not a stageable actor.

CANVAS LAYOUT (13x16 map-local squares; scene px = gx*100+400, gy*100+400): top half y 0-8 = ground floor (venarlos-home-1st-floor); bottom half y 8-16 = upper floor (venarlos-home-2nd-floor tile composited OVER a second copy of the 1st-floor image — the 2nd-floor art is opaque where it matters: wooden upstairs rooms over the WEST half, brick ROOF over the single-story EAST half). The house footprint is x 0.4-12.6, y 0.5-6.5 (ground) with street cobbles around it; front door in the south wall at about (9.2-9.7, 6.4).

ROOM IDs (Racooze redraw, keyed from furnishings vs the journal's A1-A8 list): A6 = big west room (two red sofas + west-wall stone hearth + the L-staircase cluster at its NE); A3 = oval wooden tub room top-middle; A2 = top-east room (grindstone, smith bench, hammers/tongs/blades); A1 = big east room with the ornate silver-and-gold rug, front door on the street to its south; A7 = central open room (small brazier at (6.6,2.4)); A4 = armchair + scattered white papers/books room bottom-middle-east (reads as the ransacked study); A5 = barrels/crates room bottom-middle-west; A8 = entire upper floor (bed against west wall; the Blackjack-cache closet best placed in the upstairs SE small room with desk/crates around (5.5,12.5) canvas).

A1/A7 SWAP RISK (flagged honestly): the journal's A7 is a double-height training room with lit hearth and open rafters; the only space under the tall roof-only east section big enough is the rug room — its 'Vudrani rug' could equally be read as a fencing mat, which would swap A1 and A7. I keyed the rug room as A1 because the street door opens into it and the rug matches A1's signature furnishing; A7 went to the only other open room (central hall). If your reading differs, swap those two pins — the ambush staging note below still works (the rafter assassin belongs over whichever room you call A7).

AMBUSH (default occupants — 2x Red Mantis Assassin, Moderate 80 XP @ L8): one hides in the nook UNDER the A6 stairs (token (5,2) on the under-stair shelf mass), one lurks in the A7 RAFTERS (token (7,3); it is physically above the room — give it elevation or keep it hidden until it springs when a PC climbs to A7). Both pre-buff the moment anyone enters and open by igniting both fireplaces.

Spreading House Fire hazard staged TWICE — once per burning fireplace (A6 west stone hearth (1,3); A7 brazier (6,2)); keep both hidden until the ambush triggers. If unfought, the house burns down (lockbox survives).

A½ (Amin Jalento / Gerran) is an outdoor social encounter — pin placed on the street outside the front door; Amin approaches drawn by the flames or fight. No Amin actor exists in the id map and none is required (social scene).
Floor labels follow Racooze naming: their '1st floor' = ground floor (top region), '2nd floor' = upstairs (bottom region).

Single 16x20 background image (scene px = gx*100+400, gy*100+500 — verify padding offset 400,500 for the 1600x2000 scene at 0.25 padding). House sits top-right (x 8.2-14.6, y 1.3-8.2): B1 = NW room, muddy boot-prints everywhere + empty shelves (the 'small army marched through' room); B2 = NE room, disarrayed bed against the wall with the dried-blood pillow clearly visible at (12.5,2.9); B3 = SW room, Salvator's studio — the six candle-lit skulls arc on the west desk at (8.9-10.2, 5.2-5.6), cabinet with paintbrushes/urn to the south; B4 = SE former kitchen/storeroom, riot of multicolored fungus with the thickest yellow sheets in its SW corner.

B5 = the 20-ft water-filled sinkhole bottom-left: pool spans roughly (0.5-5.5, 7.5-13.3), ringed by dark fungus clumps on its banks, with the collapsed-boardwalk timber tangle to its south at (1.5-4.5, 10.5-13.5). Mud bottom is difficult terrain; slick slope needs Athletics under pressure.

DEFAULT OCCUPANTS: Laori Vaus staged FRIENDLY in B3 amid her skull seance (she is friendly unless attacked; if first contact happens at B5 instead, she bursts out of B3 a round into the fight). 3x otyughs wallowing in the B5 sinkhole (lg 2x2 each) — journal says apply the Elite adjustment for a meaner fight. Salvator Scream is NOT here (taken to Pilts's Palace days ago) — nobody else to stage.

COMPENDIUM-LINK SUBSTITUTIONS (staged, not skipped): the journal links the official pf2e bestiary 'Otyugh (4)' and official pf2e.hazards 'Yellow Mold'; both have module-pack equivalents in the actor id map ('Otyugh' lg, actors pack; 'Yellow Mold', hazards pack), which is what the tokens reference. Yellow Mold token sits at B4's sunless NE corner per the journal (Salvator's abductors didn't know to handle samples only in sunlight).
Token sizes: Otyugh lg = 2x2 (placed at (1,9), (4,9), (3,11) — all within the water/debris of the sinkhole); Laori and Yellow Mold med 1x1.

skippedCreatures: Cinnabar — in the actor map but not staged here: she guides the Red Mantis team to the Acropolis and waits OUTSIDE the dungeon (no on-map position); she belongs to her own lair (E5).
Koriantu — not staged: he only watches the party remotely ('Koriantu is watching' via scrying); never physically present at the Acropolis.
The havero itself — no actor exists (only 'Havero Tentacle'); the part-summoned horror IS the pool floor of A2, represented by the 4 staged tentacle tokens.
designNotes: Single 44x22 background contains BOTH dungeon levels side by side: UPPER level (A1-A8) occupies map-local x 0-28; LOWER level (A9-A11) occupies x 28.5-44, separated by black void. The A5 illumacore shaft (glowing disc at ~16,17 on the upper level) feather-falls 70 ft down to the matching blue disc at (31,18) in A9's circular south chamber (levitate back up from the lower ring).

A2 is the pool chamber: balcony ring around a 15-ft-deep pool whose floor is the sleeping havero's flank; the north-south bridge at x 15.5-17 crosses it ('halfway between the east and west ends') — A2's pin sits on the bridge.

A3 observation corridors run along BOTH the north (interior y 6-7.5) and south (interior y 13.4-15.2) walls of A2 with narrow view-gaps; the pin and Krojun's band are placed in the NORTH corridor (it connects to A4's corridor). The journal does not say which gallery the Shoanti use — relocate to ~(12, 14.3) in the south corridor if preferred.

Krojun Eats-What-He-Kills + 3 Sklar-Quah Thundercallers arrived an hour before the PCs and hide at A3 to take the party's measure; staged disposition 0 (neutral) — they automatically aid against the havero.

The 4 Red Mantis Assassins actually enter ~10 minutes BEHIND the party (Cinnabar waits outside). They are staged hostile at the A1 entrance stairs as a GM convenience — keep them hidden and move them in when they strike (once discovered, or as the PCs leave).

The 4 Havero Tentacle tokens (lg = 2x2) are staged in the dark pool water of A2 but only animate at Noise Point thresholds (10 Twitch / 20 Investigate = 1 tentacle / ~30 Seek / 40 Assault = 2 / 50 Wrath = 4 max). Keep hidden until triggered. Each slain tentacle -1d20 noise; noise in A2 doubles.

A8 is pinned at the rubble-choked NORTH end of the east service corridor (interior x 22.6-23.6) — the matching collapsed SOUTH branch of the same keyed area is at approximately (23.1, 20). North led to the Thrallkeepers' quarters, south to the slave pens.

Room identification evidence: A4 = domed chamber with curved northern alcove (the Kurshu bas-relief); A5 = small south room with the rune-ringed glowing disc; A6 = south room with the bronze Lissala statue (orange-lit); A7 = six-walled angled room strewn with ancient skeletons east of A2 (six walls = six priest bas-reliefs); A10 = vault behind the 120-ton plug boulder visible at (34.7-37, 11.3-12.7) with crushed skeletons at its south end; A11 = starfield-walled room with the floating 10-ft Golarion globe at (38.6, 5.8).
Token sizes: Havero Tentacle is lg (2x2, gx/gy = top-left square); all other staged actors are med (1x1).

skippedCreatures: Truthspeaker Akram — in the actor map but arrives with the Lyrune-Quah tribe only days AFTER the reaver business; not a default occupant of this map.
Tekrakai — no bespoke actor in the id map; represented by one of the four staged Lyrune-Quah Moon Maiden tokens (renamed 'Tekrakai (Moon Maiden)').
designNotes: Two-floor composite, 40x20: floor 1 (ground, gathering hall) = left half, map-local x 0-20; floor 2 (sky well level) = right half, x 20-40. The right slot stacks a duplicate floor-1 tile UNDER the floor-2 tile so the ground floor shows through floor 2's transparent gaps — add 20 to a floor-2 local x to get composite coordinates.

B1 pin sits on the 20-ft white-stone starknife calendar emblem at the center of the ground-floor hall (the room's signature feature). The two wheel-pattern circles flanking it (x 2-6 and x 14-18) are the side towers of the facade.
B2 pin sits on the central circular chamber of floor 2 — the sky well whose moonlike sphere paints the ceiling with the true night sky at all hours.

Red Reaver (lg = 2x2 token, at ~85% HP from old wounds) staged in B1's SOUTHWEST corner where the picked-over carcasses and seven dead maidens are heaped — the journal says it lairs there. It targets the heaviest armor, pursues half a mile, fights to the death; a performance buys calm rounds (it is distracted by artistic beauty). Walls are self-repairing blessed masonry (Hardness 16).

Tekrakai's surviving band of four Lyrune-Quah Moon Maidens staged FRIENDLY outside the south approach — they brief the party (rending claws, discord-roar, distracted by beauty, never sleeps) and want in on the kill; benching them costs -2d4 RP. Loot among the dead maidens: 3 +1 hide armor, 5 +1 starknives.

I could not verify the exact position of the 30-ft-tall facade opening at available resolution; the pale approach path at roughly (9-11, 12-14) on floor 1 is the apparent south entrance — shift the maiden tokens if the implementer sees the facade facing elsewhere on the full-res map.

designNotes: Wilderness map (40x48) with no numbered area codes — a single pin 'C' links the quest page, placed on the elongated rocky cindercone outcrop on the WESTERN side of the ash flats (the journal: 'Hunt it near the western cindercones').

Cindermaw is grg (gargantuan = 4x4 token), staged surfaced on the open pale ash flat just east of the cindercone outcrop at (10,13)-(14,17). It is a roaming hunter that 'finds loiterers' — reposition freely, and stage its spark-gout surfacings before any direct encounter per the journal.

Encounter intent: the goal is the Skurak deed (one PC swallowed and out again before Shoanti witnesses), NOT a kill — slaying it costs RP ('a dead god wasn't that tough'); +2 RP for the deed, +4 if the worm lives. Severe 120 XP @ L11 if fought.
Terrain reading: dark cracked ground = cinder lands, pale regions = ash flats, dark gray curving bands (e.g. x 10-16, y 22-32) = gully/lava channels; token placement avoids the channel.

designNotes: Single-level 40x27 outdoor map: tribe camp west-center, river gorge along the west edge, octagonal horse corral (D3) center-east with gate at ~(25.5,7.7), spike-stone approach belt east.

D1 covers the whole eastern grass belt: the visible winding dirt trails through the grass east/north of camp are the unmarked safe zigzag paths (DC 31 Perception per square off-path); pin placed where the trails converge.

D5 = the largest yurt on the map, the 4-square dark-brown one SE near the corral (journal: 'the largest'; NE quarter is Ready-Klar's own) — high confidence. D4 = second-largest tan yurt at the camp's north; D6 = the distinct red-and-white striped tent SW (modest 'outsider' tent). D4/D6 are size/visual-inference judgment calls — the journal gives no compass; the unique blue yurt at (20.8,13.4) is the leading alternative for either.
D2 = the stone fire ring with central hearth, ring spans ~(10.2-13, 12.2-15); the whole camp gathers here at night (At the Foot of the Fire).

Day staging is the peaceful camp: only stageable tribe actors are the 6 thundercallers (spread through camp: fire ring, corral gate, chieftain's yurt, west camp, east lookout) and Krojun (neutral; he storms off on a long ride once the Trial is granted, returns to watch day two from the rocks).
All actors staged are medium (1x1). Skipped Assault actors: Neverfar is lg (2x2) when staged on trigger.

designNotes: 17x13 map of two grass-topped rock plateaus separated by a dark chasm. Bolt Rock has no official area codes — BR1/BR2 are invented pin codes; both link the Quest D journal page (the Trial of the Totem text).

Height assignment is a judgment call: left/west plateau = the 30-ft tier (day one), right/east plateau = the 40-ft mesa (day two) — the right formation's doubled cliff rims read as the taller summit. Swap freely if preferred; the journal only distinguishes '30-ft tier by day one, 40-ft mesa by day two'.

The 6 staged bulettes are the day-two 'Hungry Visitors' encounter (Moderate 90 XP @ L11): they burrow in with 2d4 rounds of warning and leap onto the LOWER tier, so all six are staged on the west plateau. Keep them hidden until triggered.
Bulette actor is HUGE (3x3) — placements use the top-left square of each 3x3 footprint and are non-overlapping on the tier.

The fired-clay sun totems (10 ft tall, 500 lbs, Hardness 6 HP 40) are props, not actors — the GM should add 1-3 tile/drawing markers: day one on the tier (near BR1), day two on the mesa (near BR2). A bulette passing a totem forces DC 24 Athletics or it topples.
No default occupants live here; the map is otherwise empty terrain for the endurance trial (heat, thirst, fatigue rules per the journal page).

designNotes: Single-level 60x40 map, three zones: west = brick sewer + natural caverns (E1-E4a), center = Red Mantis sanctum and Shoanti crypts (E5-E9), east = the worked Gray Maiden dungeon (E10-E21). No multi-floor tiles.

Map compass is mirrored vs several book/journal directions (E3's ledges read N/NW-curving on map, E6's mantis statue sits EAST of the chamber not west, E5's vat room lies NORTH of E6); all rooms were identified by matching furniture/contents, which is unambiguous: three blue stone vats + cot + desk = E5; six pillars + four-armed mantis statue = E6; octagonal dais sarcophagus ringed by vinegar vats = E8; white ivory-tile floor + 4 benches + gold executioner's block + axe display = E11.

Cavern flow on this map: E1 sewer (water trough x3.3-5, walkway east; rusty gate just north of the southern 'ancient collapse') -> E2 tavern (bar at north wall, bottle shelves west, two chairs south, NE door) -> E3 cavern; E3's plank bridge at (17.5-20, 8.7-12) crosses the 60-ft spike pit onto the NE ledge (the hidden Red Mantis watcher is staged beyond it); the cavern's drinkable dark pool is at (22-23.5, 13-15); cytillesh brain-fungus fields run south from E3 through (15-21, 12-19) into E4's curved NE wall.

E4 = the vast crescent cathedral cavern (5-24, 18-37): exactly nine bunk alcoves ('ceiling-height openings') scallop its S and W rim — the 2 resting assassins are staged in two of them (note they are 40 ft up; DC 26 Athletics to climb); 8 benches + pulpit at (18-23.5, 29-33); the pulpit stairs run EAST up to the mantis-facade double doors at (27-28, 30.3-32.2), which on this map open directly into E6. E5 is reached deeper in (via E6's illusion-hidden north door -> E7 corridor -> E8, with E5 off E8's SW corner).
E4a pin sits at the rubble-choked chamber NW of E4 beside E1's collapse (a bedroll + crate camp is drawn there) — moderate confidence; it is the only blocked north passage on the map.

E7 = the wide N-S corridor (30-32.5, 15-25) with three sarcophagus alcoves per side (derro cages, empty — nothing staged). E9 = the dog-leg staircase linking E10's south end down to E6's south arm at (32.5-37, 33-37): the Society's secret stair, secret doors at both ends (Boule's +15 applies). E10 = the bright ivory L-corridor (34.5-37, 19-34) from the cell block to E11's double doors.

Gray Maiden dungeon IDs: E12 = desk/cabinet office directly north of E11 sharing the windowed-iron-door wall (the dark boulder inside it is read as the commanders' secret-tunnel door, Perception DC 33). E14 = the isolated chained cell west of the north cell row (chains on wall, no visible door — matches the records-only secret cell; Marcus Endrin staged, friendly). E15 = tiled hall at (44.5-50, 19-26) with the curved barred cage (desk + filing cabinet inside) and the NW stairs up to the Longacre ground floor at (43.5-45.5, 18.5-20) — 2 guards staged in the cage, 2 in the hall. E16 = block of 8 identical bed+armor-stand rooms (40.5-48, 25.8-31.6); 4 resting guards staged in 4 rooms (on alarm they spend ~5 min armoring before joining E15). E18 = the torture chamber (43-50.5, 9-17.5): rack center-north, three cages (two on the north wall, one south), guard post strip along the west — Zenobia staged mid-indoctrination by the south cage, 3 caged recruits, 5 guards at the post.

Commander bedrooms: E21 = fur-bed room with blocky cabinet/desk and the red wall painting ('Sinister Sister'), adjacent to E18 across the corridor (matches 'Kordaitra relocating from E21'); Kordaitra staged at her desk working Boule's fake ciphers. E17 = the purple-bed/red-carpet study-bedroom beside E16, with a document basket at its south end and a display object on the north wall (warden Tisharue staged). E19 = the completely bare room with the ornate dark rug at (34.2-36.8, 10.7-13.7) ('not used in some time'). E20 = the shelf-lined room at (34.8-37.6, 13.6-17). E19/E20 are moderate-confidence by elimination — flag for review.

E20 contains a large thorny bramble mass in the map art: this is almost certainly the artist's nest for the Mother of Thorns, but the journal stations her in E11 ('attacks on sight' in the scar-carving room), so she is staged in E11 west of the executioner's block; the bramble was left unstaged. At ~40 HP she teleports to E18.

Day-pose ruses staged at hostile disposition for GM clarity: Mogmora poses as a friendly goblin barkeep behind E2's bar (dimension-doors to E6 to report after 1d4 rounds); the two manananggals wear human faces by day atop E3's ledges crying out as 'escaped prisoners'. Flip to neutral if you want the canvas to keep the secret.

Token sizes (implementer sets width/height): Catoblepas, Cytillipede, Mogmora, Vyloth = LARGE (2x2); all others staged are medium. Mogmora's lg footprint overlaps the bar — shrink to 1x1 for the goblin-form disguise if desired.

Alarm cascade (not pre-staged): everything that flees converges on E4 with Cinnabar + Koriantu behind them (Extreme 175 XP); Kordaitra + Mother of Thorns/Vyloth fallbacks stack E18 to Extreme 190 XP. Defaults are staged instead, per the day-staging brief.

E13's 12 imprisoned women are staged one per cell around the perimeter corridor (4 north row, 5 east run, 3 south row); they are beaten to 1d4 HP and cannot aid anyone (disp neutral). 15 freeable women total counting E18's 3 caged recruits.

A11/A12 collapsing-floor hazard — no hazard actor exists in the module; run from the journal's inline Checks (2d6 bludgeoning, basic Reflex DC 15, triggers when more than one Medium creature is on the floor)
designNotes: CANVAS LAYOUT: three 50x50 floor regions side by side. Map-local x 0-50 = 1st floor (ground), x 50-100 = 2nd floor (1st-floor tile re-drawn underneath, so exterior shows through), x 100-150 = 3rd floor (1st+2nd underneath). All coordinates above are in this composite frame; upper-floor pins/tokens use x+50 / x+100.

GROUND FLOOR identifications (verified by render): A5 = gargoyle-statue arch flanking the causeway bridge end (19-27, 14-16); A3 = collapsed western gate tower rubble field (4-16, 14-30, black voids = ruined rooms); A2 = the gate passage on the dirt road between the A3 ruin and the standing east tower (road runs (18-23, 16-28)); A1 = cleared dirt approach SW of the gate (8-18, 28-38; land approach enters at bottom-left); A4 = the black-topped curtain wall channel running east from the tower to the shore (29-50, 38-41; breached at 34-37; a west segment also exists at 0-9, 44-48); A6 = the wooden palisade lean-to pen against the tower's NE (26-36, 18-31; its roof is drawn on the 2nd/3rd-floor regions and hides the pen there); A7 = the central herringbone-paved vaulted storeroom full of crates/sacks (21.5-28, 28-38, central pillar) — matches A7's 'mundane supplies' treasure.

UPPER FLOORS (interpretive — the Racooze recreation has no labels; derived from journal elevations A8=20ft, A12=35ft, A11 directly above A10, A10 15-ft ceiling): 2nd-floor region: A8 = clean-paved crenellated gallery/walkways NW of the tower top (scene approx 67-77, 24-33); A9 = the cracked-but-intact dark-floored NE room (75.5-81, 28.5-33) — matches A9's 'cracks and fissures... actually safe' read-aloud; the ladder from A6 tops out at its edge (approx 78.5, 27); A10 = the staircase + cot area at (71-75.5, 31.5-35) — the cot pushed against the stairway is visible at (73-74.5, 32.5-34.5); Ury staged on it. 3rd-floor region: the small diamond tower-top room (119.5-128, 30-38.5) with open stairwell and heavily cracked sagging floor = A11 (north half, sentry staged) and A12 (south half, crenellated open battlement). If your reading differs, A9-A12 are the pins to revisit; A1-A7 are solid.

ALARM BEHAVIOR (GM staging note): on alarm the tribe redeploys — 2 orcs to A7, 3 to A8 (Ury joins, then leaps down, DC 15 Acrobatics), 1 to A12; the A11 sentry withdraws to A12 rather than trigger the floor collapse. Staged positions are the pre-alarm day posts: 1 sentry A2, 4 in A6, 1 in A11, Ury in A10 (door barred from inside, Athletics DC 27 to force).
All staged actors are Medium (1x1). Total Deadwatcher count staged = 6 + Ury, matching the journal's 'full tribe' roster.
The two square posts flanking the bridge end carry the A5 gargoyle statues (drawn as dark circles); the bridge runs north off-map toward Castle Scarwall (scene 'Scarwall - 1st Floor' B1).

designNotes: ORIENTATION: causeway enters from the south (bottom); donjon at the north (top). Single 72x93 background image, no floor compositing. This 'first floor' sheet also carries the DONJON MAIN LEVEL (F1/F2/F11/F12/F13, north end) and the WAR TOWER LOWER LEVEL (E5-E8, the NE octagonal compound at 52-66, 16-30). The donjon upper level (F3-F10, F14/F14a) and War Tower entry level (E1-E4) are drawn on the 2nd-floor map — make sure that scene's implementer picks up those pins plus Mingorc, Prelate Aruth and the F3 spectres, or they will be lost.

GATEHOUSE: B2 = the skeleton-carpeted central corridor (25-30, 70-87) through the octagonal gatehouse; B1 bridge continues south off-map (the Barbican scene sits at its far end). B3a (17-24.5, 74-84) and B3b (30.5-36.5, 74-84) flank it; the dark ribbed recesses inside them are the stairs up to C2a/C2b. B6a/B6b are read as the two unlit (black) corridors flanking the broad B4-to-B2 staircase at (22-26, 73-78) and (29.5-31.5, 73-78); west chosen as B6a — the Trench Mist (gargantuan 4x4) is staged exactly over it with the six gassed archers overlapping ('mist wells up amid the zombies'). This B6 identification is interpretive: the black areas are undrawn interiors.

LASHTON'S WELCOME: Lashton (med), Skeletal Nightmare (large 2x2) and the Skeleton Infantry troop (gargantuan 4x4 — represents the twelve skeletal champions in column) are staged inside B2's north end per 'Lashton waits here'; they sally onto B1 when intruders reach the causeway midpoint. The Skeleton Infantry journal link targets the official compendium but the module has its own actor, which is used here.

TOKEN SIZES to mind: Scarwall Guard = HUGE 3x3 (undead minotaurs — module actor size); Trench Mist & Skeleton Infantry = gargantuan 4x4; Belshallam, Corpse Lotus, Fiendish Water Elemental = huge 3x3; Skeletal Nightmare, Nessian Hell Hound, Corpse Orgy = large 2x2; Zev Ravenka = tiny. The five B17 guards only fit 4-wide cleanly in the porters' hall — the fifth overlaps and needs a nudge in Foundry.

B4 DEATH CHAMBER: great hall (roughly 14-33, 48-70 incl. side bays) strewn with preserved corpses and gore. The Corpse Orgy is staged on the large drawn corpse mound at (26-29.5, 66.5-69.5); the journal says 'northwest corner' but the map art puts the only mound at the hall's south end — followed the art, GM may relocate.

B5 MANDRAIVUS'S FALL: the bone-choked north-south hallway at (31.5-34.5, 43-62) east of B4 — the whole skeleton in dust-caked full plate is clearly visible at its north end (33-35, 44-47); Mandraivus token placed on it, Vortex of Madness hazard mid-hall. The double door barred from the B5 side is in its west wall to B4.

SERVICE BLOCK WEST: B15 kitchens (20-26, 44-50) — the three dome ovens are the arc shapes along the west wall (x 20.5-21.5); Crematory Blast hazard staged at the oven fronts and the four cinder ghouls on the oven wall (they peel off the brick only after the haunt — GM may keep them hidden). B16 larder = small debris room SW at (19-26, 50-52); B20 wine cellar = the unlit room west of the kitchens (18-20, 44-52). B16/B20 interiors are not drawn (black) — least certain pins on the sheet.

SERVICE BLOCK EAST (door-chain verified): B17 porters' hall = the long room south of the courtyard (34-41, 41-47; its north wall carries the arrow slits onto B21) -> double door -> B18 workshop (37.5-42, 47-52.5, dusty workbenches; Polong Bottles hazard at the three southern doors) -> B19 Malatrothe's lair (35-42, 52.5-56; bags of netting and her glowing brazier visible — Malatrothe staged beside it).

QUARTERS: east side along B5 — B9 (scroll-covered table, 36-41, 56-58.5), B10 (cross-marked bunk room, 32-35.5, 56-58.5; Knurlott staged sitting there), B8 (the bunk-in-splinters room matching its read-aloud, 32-36, 60.5-64.5), B7 (big room with oversized minotaur bunks + Gralka's gore, 31-40, 64.5-70 — her undead-bane obsidian battleaxe loot is here). West side mirror: B11 (bunks, 13-19, 48-56), B12 (16, 59), B13 (18, 64), B14 (18.5, 68.5). B11-B14 internal divisions are best-fit from furniture; B7/B8 are read-aloud-confirmed.

B21 COURTYARD: garden (20-41, 29-40); the 30-ft oubliette well is the stone ring at (20-22, 37-39); Corpse Lotus (3x3) staged in the vegetation beside it — it never leaves the courtyard. The twin ribbed stair banks at the garden's north edge rise 15 ft to the donjon platform (F1 doors at the top, sealed with lead + stone shape).

B22/B23/B24: B22 = the wagon-filled livery (26-31.5, 41-52) per its read-aloud; all four tenebrous worms staged there (an alternative silk-reading exists: the web-covered strip north of B23 at 41-50, 26-31 — GM may move the worms). B23 = the vast gutted stable hall (44-58, 30-52: dung heaps, burned beams, pillar stubs); Belshallam (3x3) staged sleeping center-west — Perception DC 10 to wake; read his journal entry before running (compulsion, stillness signal, craven turn). B24 = the loot-pile alcove in the lair's SE corner (54-58, 45-50); no separately walled vault is visible, pin approximate.

DONJON MAIN LEVEL: F2 = the red-tiled star-shaped foyer (15-30, 4-17) — the 10-ft pool is the blue circle at (23-26, 11.5-14.5), Fiendish Water Elemental (3x3) staged dormant over it (erupts 3 rounds after a living creature enters unless the Kuthite cleansing ritual is performed); the skull altar is the dark fixture on the foyer's east side. F11 = the gray slate hall with obsidian pillars and dim torches (25-37, 14-28); Zev Ravenka (tiny, inert remains) staged on the altar-like fixture at (31, 22) — adjust to the black altar if it reads differently in Foundry; he is a SPIRIT ANCHOR and stays inert until his remains are disturbed. F12 refectory = octagonal table with place settings (37.5-42, 15-20); F13 library = reading-desk room (34-40, 9-14.5). F1 = sealed bronze double doors at the courtyard-stairs platform (pin 35.5, 27.5). FORBIDDANCE wards (Will DC 34, 6d6 mental) apply to the whole donjon footprint — see the F page intro. F11a (curtain alcove) and F14a (vault) are sub-areas not separately pinned; F14a is on the upper level anyway.

WAR TOWER LOWER LEVEL (NE octagonal compound, reached by the walled corridor/stair from the donjon's east corridor): E5 = the eastern hall scorched with black burn marks (58-65, 18-27) — both Nessian hell hounds (2x2, Elite) staged here; they wander the lower floors and their howling alerts the E3 guards (2nd-floor scene) and Gorstav. E6 = collapsed-bunk room NW (53-58, 18-25, difficult terrain; adamantine dagger + buckler on a DC 20 search). E7 = south chamber (57-63, 26-30): Risibeth the sayona staged at disposition NEUTRAL (she is a social encounter first — 'blood for history') with the Insanity Mist Chest hazard on the right-hand chest. E8 = SW armory (52-57, 25-30, slumped weapon racks; the three cursed bait weapons).

Unkeyed verticals worth noting for walls/doors pass: round stair turret in B23's west wall (41.5-45.5, 36-42), SE spiral stair (38.5-42, 58.5-63.5), round west tower with ring corridor (8.5-13, 56.5-62) — all lead to upper floors. The guest-wing connection leaves the map's west edge around (0-14, 26-36); B25-B37 pins belong to the 'Scarwall - Guest Wing' scene.

G7 Chamber of the Well (four artery-pillars, 10-ft mist-filled shaft, Ildervok invisible above the well) lies 50 ft ABOVE G8 with the same star footprint and is NOT drawn separately — both pins sit in the star chamber. Ildervok (huge 3x3) is staged there but canonically belongs to G7: GM runs the star room as G7 first (curate confrontation / Devil-You-Know turn), then the party drops through the mist shaft and the same room doubles as G8.

H1 = the long winding lightless tunnels from the star chamber to the southern shelf. H2 pin marks the small ring-loop feature at (6.5-8, 33.5-35) read as the 15-ft shaft dropping 500+ ft to Sekamina — MODERATE confidence (it is the only pit-like feature along the tunnels).

H3: the southern rocky shelf carries exactly four 20-ft domed hovels — (22.5-27.5,33.5-39.5), (25.5-30,28-33), (29-31.5,39.5-42.5 — embers inside), (28-31.5,43-47). One Gug (lg 2x2) staged inside each; they drop everything, rush intruders, fight to the death, and hurl javelins/howl for Kleestad against fliers. The plank dock at (29-33, 33.5-36.5) is the launch point onto the lake.

H4: Serithtial is the glowing sword point-down on the main island (light visible at ~42,27) — an item, not an actor, so no token. Kleestad (gargantuan 4x4) staged submerged in open water just west of the island at (34,27); he surfaces the moment anyone takes to the water, touches the sword-rock, or grabs the blade, and pursues to the end of the world if he survives.
Lake-bed treasure (~200 ft down: staff of life, rod of wonder, speed battleaxe) has no map feature — journal only.
Sword note for the GM: Serithtial stays suppressed (masterwork blade) until Mithrodar falls and the curse lifts.

designNotes: TILE STACK: this scene layers scarwall-second/third/fourth-floor.webp (each 7200x9300) all at the SAME position, scene px (1800,2400) = map-local origin (padding offset 18,24 squares). They are whole-castle overlays, not side-by-side floors: each higher image is mostly transparent and repaints only what changes at that storey, so the composite reads as the 4th-storey view with lower storeys showing through. All coordinates given are map-local squares per the renderer labels.

Keyable 4th-storey content is sparse — most of the image is rooftop. E14 = the War Tower's octagonal roof battlement (x52.7-64.8, y17.6-29.6) with the journal's three wrecked siege engines; the inner diamond room (x53-57.5, y22.5-30, stairs + round table) is just the stairhead coming up from the E9-E13 level (which Racooze draws on the 3rd floor) — no separate code. G1 = the Star Tower's raised diamond rooftop (x17.5-26.5, y8.5-15.5); the skull-and-chains phase-door carving is the dark organic blotch at ~(25.6,15.1) — pin placed on it. D16 = the open crenellated octagon perched atop the main keep (x25.4-29, y54.8-59.2); it has NO stair or hatch (journal: 'no stair connects the cupola to the keep') and the 5th-floor image roofs it with a cone — clearly the cupola. Aerilaya staged pacing it (med 1x1).

The big circular drum at x22-31, y38-49 is the Tower of Kazavon, but its interior (rain pool, chain-slung bed, gilt-throne dais) is painted on the 2nd/3rd-floor images and merely shows through the 4th tile's transparent center — only the rim and decorative spikes are 4th-image. D14's pin and Nihil's token belong to the 'Scarwall - 3rd Floor' scene; do not duplicate here. Likewise D1/D2 (gatehouse loft + turrets): the 4th image shows only the gatehouse roof/parapet and bare turret tops; the keyed interiors are on the 3rd floor.

GARGOYLES (D8): AP text = 'a pair atop each of the four minarets' (8 total). I identified the four hollow minaret tops on this image as: W ring (center ~12,61), E ring (center ~42,61) — both open shafts with cross-braces, capped on the 5th image — and the twin small drums at (36.8-38.8, 45.8-50.3) NE of the cupola. 8 Gargoyle Brutes staged as pairs on those four tops; the D8 base chambers (3rd floor) are empty by default, so this staging is the flock's day position. Identification is my best reading of the art; if re-judged, keep pairs together. A D8 convenience pin was placed at the W minaret.
D13 is never described in the module journal (omitted/merged upstream); nothing could be pinned for it on any floor.
All staged tokens are medium (1x1). Racooze logo tile sits at map-local (40,85)-(46,92.5) — ignore it.

B28 = the long banquet-table hall (table x46-54, y14-16). B27 = the room with pale oversized (minotaur) bunks at (53.5-60.5, 19.5-24); its NW door (~54.5,19.5) opens on the corridor that hides B28's east-wall secret door — that door triggers the minotaur phantasm. B25 = the large bare hall NE (x58-64, y9.5-17) on the way in from the castle (torn tapestries; Orcish blood-warning on its west wall).

Baths: B30 = the BIG south pool (x50.8-55.5, y27.9-31.4 — 'fills much of the room'); all six Festering Spirits staged IN this pool (they rise at first touch or when the party turns to leave). B32 = the smaller north pool (x49.6-52, y22.8-26.3), its 'algae' staged as the Green Slime hazard token on the water. B31 = the narrow room west of B32 with double bunks lining the wall (~46.3-47.6, 24.7-27.6).
B33's 'seven identical rooms' = the small guest chambers through the wing's center (north row ~x30.5-48, y9.5-13.5 and southern row ~x31-46, y18-26); single cluster pin placed at a northern chamber.

Honest uncertainties: B25 vs B27 could conceivably swap with neighboring bare rooms; the furnished SE corner room (x57.5-62, y27.5-32, round table + bed) is left unkeyed — likely a B33-style southern guest room. Everything else matched read-aloud details directly.

Pegg & Loute: the community conversion stats the duo as ONE medium actor (AP had two separate CR6 ghosts), so a single token is staged in B26 — GM may rename/duplicate cosmetically but XP is budgeted for the pair. Tarnworm is GARGANTUAN (4x4): token top-left (0,28) puts it in open water SW of the quay ('surges at anything within 20 ft of shore'); B37's pin sits on the ruined wooden quay at (0.5-2.5, 22.3-23.2). Danse Macabre floats camouflaged against the rose-glass ceiling over the ballroom center; all tokens except the Tarnworm are medium (1x1).
Single background image; renderer map-local coords = scene coords. Map content spans roughly x0-33, y0-31 of the 35x35 canvas.

Eight identical coffer-vault chambers exist: N (12.2,3.3) and (19.5,3.2); W (9.4,12.8) and (9.2,19.3); E (26,11.3), (31,9.7), (31,13.2), (30.7,17.2). Journal = A1 + 'seven chambers like A1' (A4). A1 assignment is inferred (chose the vault opening directly off A2's north side); the A4 pin sits on the east cluster representative — the other six chambers are also A4.

Two pillared halls: west hall (6.5-15.5, 4.5-9.7) has the gutted chests/urns/remains = A2. The east hall (17.3-22.5, 5-13.5, orange-stained, pillared) is not separately keyed in the journal — treat as part of A2/treasury annex.

A10 'three collapsed passages' = the three swirl-collapse blobs at (23.3,1.8) NE, (30.5,21.3) E, and (23.6,28.5) SE; pin placed on the NE blob. A7 (bricked passage with Ileosa's smashed hole) = the separate west corridor dead-end at (4.5,10.9) — journal calls it 'southwest', fan-map drew it west; flagged as my best match.

A8: the four 'crumbling kneeling-servant statues' read as the crumbled rubble band along the south edge of the octagonal tomb room (8.5-11.5, 28.3-29.5), warding the white dais+sarcophagus (A9, center 9.9,26.3). Paralytic Pulse hazard token placed in that band; the 4 Shining Children coalesce only when the trap triggers — implementer should place them HIDDEN. Journal links the official monster-core Shining Child, but the module's own 'Shining Child' actor (id GxMQ4GpCnVKmEt3S) exists in the id map, so I staged that; drop the 4 tokens if the convention is to leave trap-spawns unstaged.
A3 Royal Crypts confirmed by the five upright sarcophagus niches along the room's south wall (x≈14.7,16.7,18.7,20.7,22.3 at y≈29.3) — the fifth, slightly apart, suits 'and (apart) Cardraith'.
A5 confirmed: circular chamber (15.3-19.8, 14.2-18.8) with the huge greenish-gray central pillar (the sealed plug); SE rubble matches the read-aloud collapse.

A11: Venster's purple-clad corpse is actually drawn visible in the room (27.3-28.3, 4.9-6.6) even though the text walls it behind a new brick wall (west side of the room, off the lit corridor at ~24.5,5.5) — GM may want a wall/door there.
Round water-bottomed shaft at (14.1,22.3) is the bottom of the shaft that also appears at the same map-local spot on the 1st and 2nd floor maps (pinned as A22 oubliette on the 1st floor).
Secret spiral A6 at (24.2,15.2) aligns vertically with 1st-floor A24 spiral (23.7,15.8) and 2nd-floor A36 spiral (23.4,15.6) — the A6-A24-A36 secret route is stacked across the three scenes.
No living default occupants on this level besides the A8 trap encounter — all other rooms are looted vaults/crypts.
