designNotes: Single background image; renderer map-local coords = scene coords.

Main approach chain verified: A12 outdoor stairs (2.7-9, 2.8-7) climb east into the NW arch tower (interior 9.8-12.5, 4.5-7); the tower's upper chamber on the 2nd-floor map is A26 (portcullis kill-box), from which a wide stair continues toward the 3rd floor — consistent with 'main stairs A12 / entrance hall A46' intel.
A13 Chapel confirmed by blue pews, two side altars, candle rows and the white robed Aroden statue in the south apse (room 11-19, 4.5-11).

A14 confirmed by the tiered semicircular DRY FOUNTAIN against the east wall at (23.6,8.2). Its 'locked stair to A40' = the rust-red barred gate at (24.3,5.5) opening onto the L-shaped stair (26.5-29.5, 4.5-7.5), which lands at the 2nd-floor NE landing room I pinned as A40 — vertical alignment checked.
A15 = the grand spiral staircase (25-33.5, 15.5-22.5); the same shaft appears at the same map-local spot on the 2nd-floor map.

Prisons: ten floor-grate cages are split between two cage rooms (4.3-9.3, 11.5-15) and (4.3-9.3, 15.5-19). A19/A20 room order and A21 (the jailer's bedroom with corpse-on-bed at 11-13.7, 15.5-19.5) are positional inferences — the journal lumps 'A19-A22 the prisons' in one sentence and only specifies Veen's cage (A20) and the oubliette (A22).

A22 = the round stone pit ring at (14.2,22.3). Caveat: the identical ring appears directly above on the 2nd floor and below on the dungeon map (water glint at the bottom), so it could be read as a castle well; I kept it as the oubliette since it is the only pit-like cell on the floor and the journal's A22 is 'the oubliette'.

A23 = the refuse room (25-29.7, 11.8-14.2) with rot/moss draining through the zigzag exterior passage on the NE cliff; it sits directly below the 2nd-floor scalloped latrine tower (three privy holes at ~31.5-32.5, 11.8-13.8 on the 2nd-floor map) — the garderobe soil shaft.
A24 confirmed by the firewood stacks at (22.1-23.2, 18.3-19.6); the secret A6-A24-A36 spiral is in the adjoining alcove at (23.7,15.8), aligned with the dungeon and 2nd-floor spirals.

A17 Lower Guardroom (16-24.5, 22-29.3): central table + armor/weapon racks match the read-aloud. 6 Elite Erinyes staged, but per the journal roll 1d6 for how many are physically home (the rest are greater-teleport spot-checking the floor). Ileosa's Fury is present on the party's FIRST assault only and does not fight — she teleports to the Sunken Queen to report; remove her token on later assaults.

A18 Justice Chamber (5-13, 22-29.5): long table + 4 chairs + stool, torture rack, pulley wheel and upright iron-maiden all visible. Akaruzug is LARGE (2x2) and starts INERT against the southwest wall with Ishani Dhatri's corpse crucified to it. Vavana Dhatri staged here per the A18 encounter block.
Unkeyed: SW round tower (void interior), two black closets north of the chapel (likely the f2 stairwell shaft), wide stair down at (16-18.2, 29.8-32.4) matching the 2nd-floor stair above it.

skippedCreatures: Hamatula (1d3, summoned by the Yallops round 1) — official compendium link (pf2e.pathfinder-bestiary-2) and summon-only, not a default occupant
Pudgyknuckles — only a 30% random kitchen visitor (lairs on the 4th floor); not staged here
Gray Maiden patrol (4x Palace Guard, 10% per 5 minutes on 2nd/3rd floors) — wandering encounter, not staged
Imp housekeepers (two dozen, invisible, castle-wide) — ambient flavor, not staged
Green slime urns (A26 murder holes) and Togomor's silent mental alarm (A28) — no hazard actors exist in the module for these
designNotes: Scene uses one map tile at scene px (900,900); all coordinates above are MAP-LOCAL squares per the renderer labels — implementer must add the 9-square padding offset when converting to scene coords.

A26 = the NW tower upper chamber (9.3-13, 4-6.2); the portcullis grille is the studded line on its east side at x≈12.2. A54 (3rd floor) with the murder holes sits directly above. The wide stair east of it (13.3-14.7, 3.3-6.3) continues the A12 main approach upward. A27 'Bolted Door' pinned on the inner guard room (10.2-13, 6.3-8.2) behind the gate — the exact bolted door is not distinguishable on the art; flagged as inference.

A28 Armory is a two-room complex: main hall (25.3-29.7, 21.8-29) with the long table (ladder stowed beneath it per the journal) and weapon racks both sides, plus the rack-filled west annex (19.8-24.3, 22.5-27.3). The two locked secret doors connect the complex inward; exterior door 15 ft up is on the outer wall. Remember Togomor's silent alarm: opening the outer door summons the Yallops in 1d3 rounds.
A29 pinned on the SE corner tower spiral (adjacent to the armory, matching its A28-A29 numbering); the SW corner tower has a matching unkeyed spiral at (4.3,29.7).

A30 vs A38: two bed clusters exist — NW pair of rooms (4.8-10.5, 10.8-16.3) and the south-center suite (13-17.5, 23.5-31, with figures sleeping in beds). Assigned A30=NW, A38=south by numbering flow; 4 Palace Guards staged in each per '4 present in each at any hour'. The small white critter drawn by the A38 bed (14.5,25.5) is map art (cat), not Pudgyknuckles.

A37 Mess Hall confirmed exactly by the read-aloud: four stone pillars, four food-piled tables, wrecked chairs, antler/barb trophies, bottle shelf and hearth. The Yallops' major-image hellscape is not on the art. Morale: any Yallop at <=20 HP or one touch of Serithtial sends all three teleporting to Sermignatto (A59, 3rd floor).

Togomor staged gorging on the bed in A44 (canopied bed, round exotic carpets, three narrow NE windows all visible on the art). If the A12 stair alarm rings first he pre-buffs and dimension-doors to A45 instead — GM relocates. His wall cache is above the bed (Perception DC 34).
A36 confirmed: spiral stair at (23.4,15.6) aligns with 1st-floor A24 (23.7,15.8) and dungeon A6 (24.2,15.2) — the secret A6-A24-A36 route; the stair top is hidden (Perception DC 38).

A32-A35 and A39-A42 are covered by ONE journal sentence ('pantry, workshops, smithy, stores; the Archive (A41)'), so individual code placement is positional inference matched to visible function: jar-shelf pantry rooms east of the kitchen (A32/A33), long serving counter south of the mess (A34), forge room (A35 smithy — the dwarf-shaped anvil is the journal's detail), lumber workbench (A39), NE landing room with well-head where the locked A14 stair arrives (A40), cabinet+desk+ledger room (A41 Archive), bench workroom east of A44 (A42). Treat these eight pins as best-effort.
The scalloped NE tower room with three privy holes (31.5-32.5, 11.8-13.8) is the garderobe over the 1st-floor A23 refuse room — useful for GMs tracking the gaseous-entry route.
The well/oubliette shaft ring appears at (13.7,21.8) beside the kitchen — same shaft as 1st-floor A22 and the dungeon ring.

skippedCreatures: Barbed Devil x3 (hamatulas) — summoned by Mavrokeras on round 1 of the A45 set-piece; not present by default
Mavrokeras — fights the A45 set-piece from the A67 balcony overhead (4th Floor); token staged at his default roost A94 on the Towers scene
Zarmangarof — A65 is his nest (pinned on the 4th Floor scene) but his keyed encounter is Event 2 (city overflight with Sabina); the nest stands empty during the assault
Erinyes alarm patrols — keyed to A17 on the 1st Floor scene, not this floor
designNotes: Single map tile (castle-korvosa-3rd-floor.webp, 35x35). All coords are map-local squares; verified against high-zoom crops of the source image (same 100px/sq coordinate system as the renderer).

Gatehouse orientation: the castle gate is the NW apse structure. A54 Gatehouse Lower Gallery = the long apse-ended hall (x9-13, y1-11): its long brown wall objects are missile-weapon racks, the four round urns are the green-slime urns (hazard token placed at one), the dark floor squares are the murder holes over A26, and the west alcoves are the arrow slits covering the A12/A45 approach. The 5 Gray Maidens are posted at those west embrasures.

A45 = the flagstone landing (x16-19.5, y3.5-7.5) where the main stair turns; the broad south stair rises into the A46 bust-lined entrance corridor (x11-24, y11.3-14.2). The A46 guard pair stands before the southern doors.

High-confidence IDs by furnishings: A51 (jousting lances at x12-14.2,y17.7), A52 (checkered hall, granite dais + Crimson Throne at x5.8-8.7,y23.2), A55 (two-story theater, wooden stage x18.5-23.5,y22-24.3), A56 (green spiral-pattern room with spellbooks on the table), A58 (white-marble round tub with flowers), A59 (red canopied bed N, desk+armchair S; shares its north wall with the stage = peephole), A63 (round pool in mid-room, side basins).

Best-fit IDs (flagged honest): A47 = wardrobe-comb room (29.5-32, 6-8.5); A49 = twin-pillared room east of the stair (19.7-24.3, 3.7-10.3); A50 = room with two rows of stored chairs (26.5-31, 11-14.5); A53 = NW chairless carved-table room with stairs up (3.5-9.5, 9-13.5); A57 = room with treasure sparkle/sideboard east of A59 (26-30.5, 23-26.5); A60 = small sparse bedroom (23.5-26.5, 13.5-17); A64 = NE octagon mezzanine with round table+chairs (the level below the A69 Game Room). If A50/A53 feel swapped in play, swap the pins — evidence was split.

Gray Tower (SW round, x0.5-7.5, y27-34.5) is drawn mostly open: A61 pinned at the landing room+door joining the A32-down stairs and the tower ascent; A62 pinned in the tower's south chamber — treat that area as the dimensionally-locked cell (cell walls are not clearly drawn).

Togomor is staged here per the A45 layered-defense (real body in A54, project image at the stair-head); his fallback default A44 is on the 2nd Floor scene — if both scenes are staged, use one. Below ~30 HP he dimension-doors to A59.
Sermignatto (lg 2x2) staged in A59 = his manifestation point; he is actually ethereal until Togomor dies or the PCs reach this room. Nessian Hell Hounds are lg (2x2).
False Ileosa idles in the royal suite (A57-A59) between alarms; staged on the throne per the keyed A52 court scene. She melts into blood at 0 HP (variant simulacrum).

skippedCreatures: Mavrokeras — A67 is only his invisible ambush perch for the A45 set-piece (plus 20% spot chance per balcony transit at A67/A70/A76); staged at his roost A94 on the Towers scene
designNotes: Scene stacks the 3rd-floor tile beneath the 4th-floor tile at the same origin; the 4th-floor image is transparent over the grand stairwell, the stage-hall dome and the open terraces, so the 3rd floor correctly shows through (these are open two-story spaces). Map-local coords work identically on both tiles.

Two areas from the A45-A66 journal page are physically drawn on this map (mezzanine level over the tall third floor) and pinned here: A65 = the NW apse hall with the black dragon's nest of rags at the apse end, armory weapon-rack niches, directly above A54 (its torn ceiling leads up to the A79 floor gap); A66 = the trophy gallery north of the salon (mounted antlered head, skeleton specimen, web-pattern rugs). Conversely A64 (Break Room) is drawn on the 3rd-floor map (NE octagon) and pinned there.

A67 = NW terrace with catapult + stacked shot overlooking the open stairwell/gate approach. A70 = small east balcony with one light catapult, directly atop the A48 garderobe shaft. A76 = the L-shaped south terrace (catapults + shot piles), linking Gray/Seawatch tower tops to the salon.

A71 = the open-topped NE corridor between the Game Room wing and the salon's NE door, flanked by sloping roofs; its assassin clings to the roof above the salon door at the corridor's west end (token placed there). Moderate confidence on which salon door — the AP only says 'above the A72 door'.

A72 Grand Salon: the five assassins lair in hammocks 25 ft up along the ceiling, screened by hanging silks — tokens are spread on the salon floor beneath their hammock positions (no alarm: 1d4 asleep aloft, the rest hidden at the walls; alarmed: all five at ceiling height). A73 = the enclosed bar room with the netting hammock over its tables; Kayltanya sleeps there and joins the A72 fight the instant it erupts (met alone at A73 only if every alarm was avoided).

A74 = facing red benches + hookah side-tables across the fretwork-floored hall; A75 = the west half-round turret with red window seats and tea table (sunset view). These two could conceivably be swapped, but the bench layout strongly favors this reading.
A77 = the bare thick-walled room jutting from the south terrace; arcane-locked iron door, permanent phase door in the ceiling 20 ft up (Arabasti symbol) to A90.
Scene stacks 3rd + 4th floor tiles beneath the attic tile (same origin); lower floors correctly show through the open air around the shrunken attic footprint.

NE cluster verified in detail: A88 = bed/table/stove/showcase room (25.5-30, 3-8) with the melted stone trapdoor down to A69; A89 = the hidden chamber east of it (30-32.5, 3-8) with bed, one-legged round table, silver lantern and harrow deck — Venster's token is his manifestation point (a weak mist until his bones from A11 are returned; he is a redemption NPC, disposition neutral). Topology matches the AP exactly: A86 junk room (diagonal, 23-30, 9.5-14.5) -> hidden NE door -> A87 cobwebbed 5-ft crawl (26.5-30.5, 8-9.5) -> Venster's rooms.

A79 = the N strip (9-12.5, 2-11) with lumber/slate piles and the torn floor gap at its north end overlooking the main entrance (Zarmangarof's access to A65 below). A80 = west room with dyed-yarn spools and crates; A82 = SW room with pale grain sacks.
A78/A81 occupy the long west strip (casks in its north half, mattress in its south half); the dividing wall on the redraw is faint, so those two pins are approximate within the strip.
A85 pinned on the gray rooms with stacked cage-like crates between the main attic and the Epochal approach — moderate confidence (the AP only says half the room is pigeon cages).

Bridge posts: each assassin hides in shadows near the tower end of her bridge (tokens placed there); unless spotted they let PCs pass and stalk them, and flee to A72 if discovered. A83a runs SSW from the west wing to the Gray Tower top; A83b runs south from the Epochal Tower to the Seawatch top.

A84's narrow SE hallway leads to the large circular stair ascending the Epochal Tower to A91/A92 (on the Towers scene); the boxed structure in the middle of A84 is the lantern dome over the salon/stage shaft, and the bottom-center square structure is Domina's Tower passing through (no attic entrance — sealed).

Single background (castle-korvosa-towers.webp, 25x30): five separate tower-top circles serve six keyed areas. Identification: top-left gear-shaped shell with scaffolds/planks/tools = A92 (Eodred II's unfinished grander-clock); top-right circle with bookshelf blocks and three treasure sparkles = A90 (the sparkles match its three treasure clusters: invisible contract tube on the top shelf, planar scrolls, fool's-leprosy potion rack); mid-right rocky-rimmed circle with the great gear ring and central winding capstan = A91 (hand-wound monthly by Mavrokeras); mid-left bare circle with small spiral stair = A95 (abandoned; stair down to the A83a bridge level); bottom four-bastioned circle = Seawatch.

Seawatch is drawn once but holds two keys: A93 (guard-post level — pinned at the spiral stair that descends toward A83b) and A94 (the lookout level above, reached by ladder — pinned at the north battlement). The GM should treat the same circle as either level as the party climbs.

A90/A91 disambiguation rests on the treasure sparkles and the no-stairs rule for A90 (reachable only via the A77 phase door / teleport after first visit); flag for QA if it reads wrongly at the table.

Mavrokeras (lg, 2x2) staged at A94, his keyed roost (Low 60 XP @ L15): he patrols the castle air ring-invisible (4th-rank at-will), fronts the A45 set-piece from the A67 balcony, and returns here below 30 HP to regenerate before circling again. His gear (Flesh Shredder spiked chain, ring of invisibility) is embedded on the actor.
A92's trapdoor down to A91 and the A94 catwalk door both carry arcane locks (Thievery DC 39); A95 and the Epochal stair are viable climbing routes through the castle.

notes.label: B20. The Everdawn Pool
notes.pageName: B20: THE EVERDAWN POOL — the Finale

skippedCreatures: Kazavon Reborn — in the actor map but deliberately not staged: he only manifests if the 10-round 'Rise of the Dragon' event completes after Ileosa's defeat; GM drops the gargantuan (4x4) token at the Pool (map-local ~50,21) only then.
designNotes: Canvas layout (map-local 100px squares, origin = renderer labels): 1st/flooded level image occupies x0-18, y2-25; 2nd/surface level x18-41, y1-26; 3rd-4th composite image x41-64, y0-27 — within it the 3rd-level suite is the top structure (~x46-58, y2.5-13.5) and B20 (4th level) is the bottom square room (~x48.7-56.7, y18.3-24.7). NOTE: the scene's Foundry grid is 50px/5ft; all coordinates here are in the renderer's 100px label units (= 10ft, matching the AP's 'one square = 10 feet'), so the implementer must multiply by 100px and may place tokens on half-grid only via these integer 100px squares.

Room identifications were cross-checked against the original AP p.381 level diagrams (extracted text layout), not just the art: L1 has the N-S axis B6(N, pillared silt chamber)→B2 (glowing 15-ft crystal globe between two square pillars — pin sits on the globe)→B1 (four-pillar hall with central silt mound); B5 = NW room littered with pottery shards/rusted metal (pile in its east, lynx mosaic on NW wall); B4 = SW room with the rotting-algae bed and the mangled-alligator/boggard bone pile (clearly drawn); B3 = the organic mud-bulb tunnel Beirawash dug east of B1.

L2: B7 = the south corridor that pierces the outer wall into the mangroves at map (25.2,19.7) (Ileosa's disintegrate-cut door + illusory wall — sentry token stands mid-hallway); B8 = west camp room (straw bedrolls, fishing rods on the level wooden platform; 5 champions staged there); B9 = NW stores (barrel, chest, hanging gar fillets, ladder); B10 = north angled-shaft glyph; B11 = NE red-algae water room; B12 pin sits on the visible crystal-tube sparkle in the central flooded gallery; B13 = floating barrel platform on the east side; B14 = south flooded shaft passage (angled-shaft glyph at y17.4).

Racooze draws the inter-level shafts as angled 'stair' glyphs: the B6↔B10 shaft glyph appears at the same relative spot on both L1 (map ~(8-10, 7.3-8.6), just S of B6 — matching 'archway in the southwest ceiling') and L2 (map ~(28.4-30, 6.8-7.4)). B14's glyph rises to B15's SW shaft; the stair glyph N of the pink hall (map ~(51.4-53.8, 4.9-5.5)) is the B15→B20 shaft; B20's floor opening to it is the dark rectangle at map (51-54, 19-20.8). The buried ancient stairway S of B1 (map ~(8.5-10.5, 20.5-23)) is the read-aloud 'tilted stairway dropping into a horizontal layer of silt' — it is NOT an inter-level connection (the AP is explicit: shafts only).

L3: B15 = central rose-marble hall with eight crystal-tube nodes (Ithier staged here, fights to the death, pursues everywhere except flooded levels); B16 = NW room (strap-fitted chairs/benches on level platform — the four named furies staged here as their day posts; they teleport to Ileosa the instant she is attacked, and are also her response team vs B2 looters); B17 = SW workshop (anvil, benches, half-finished ring gates); B18 = SE bedroom (bed, wardrobe, harp); B19 = NE red square basin (Immortal Ichor token in the basin; Soultrapping Gem hazard token on the gold gem-pedestal just S of the basin rim).

B20 staging: Ileosa's token sits at the Pool's center — she floats in semi-trance INSIDE the 30-ft blood blob (consider elevation ~10ft and hidden-until-noticed; very stealthy parties get a surprise round at the Pool's center). The 6 False Ileosa simulacrums and the Taniniver are Last-Battle spawns ('unleashed'/birthed by the Pool round 1) — implementer should set those 7 tokens hidden until combat starts. Surviving furies teleport in and Ithier climbs up if alive; pull their tokens over then. The teal bands on B20's south wall are the blue-crystal 'eyes' windows.

Token sizes: Beirawash is GARGANTUAN (4x4, staged at map 7,15 covering the west-center of B1's hall); Ithier and Taniniver are HUGE (3x3); all other staged actors incl. the Soultrapping Gem hazard are Medium 1x1.
